zayed_mosque.title: Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque
zayed_mosque.location: Sheikh Rashid Bin Saeed Street, Abu Dhabi City
zayed_mosque.hours: Sat–Thu 09:00–22:00 (last admission 21:30); Fri 09:00–12:00 and 15:00–22:00 (closed during Friday morning prayers)
zayed_mosque.fee: Free; a free online e-pass is required to enter
zayed_mosque.context: One of the world's largest mosques, with gold-plated Swarovski chandeliers, the world's largest hand-knotted carpet, and four minarets standing 106 metres tall. Free guided tours run daily (~45 minutes, departing from the East side); 'Sura' night tours operate 22:00–09:00. Modest dress is required — abayas are available to borrow on-site and women must cover their hair inside. Hours may differ during Ramadan and special events.
zayed_mosque.nudge: Plan around prayer times and dress modestly — then continue to the Wahat Al Karama memorial nearby.

louvre.title: Louvre Abu Dhabi
louvre.location: Saadiyat Cultural District, Saadiyat Island, Abu Dhabi
louvre.hours: Tue–Thu 10:00–18:30; Fri–Sun 10:00–20:30; closed Mondays. The Dome is free to all until midnight daily
louvre.fee: Adults AED 65; under 18 free; People of Determination free (plus one companion); guided tours AED 50–130
louvre.context: A universal museum under Jean Nouvel's iconic floating dome, displaying world-class art spanning civilisations and centuries. Fully accessible with step-free access and a complimentary wheelchair loan, and home to the Fouquet's French brasserie. The Dome stays open and free to all until midnight. Part of the Saadiyat Cultural District — a Museums Pass offers good value if visiting multiple venues.
louvre.nudge: Pair your visit with neighbouring Saadiyat Cultural District museums for a full day of art.

qasr_al_watan.title: Qasr Al Watan
qasr_al_watan.location: Al Ras Al Akhdar, Abu Dhabi City
qasr_al_watan.hours: 11:00–18:15 daily; the 'Palace in Motion' evening show runs after hours (last entry 30 min before the show)
qasr_al_watan.fee: AED 65 (includes the palace, the Palace in Motion show, shuttle bus, library and free parking); UAE seniors 65+ 60% off; People of Determination free
qasr_al_watan.context: The UAE Presidential Palace, open to the public — an interactive journey through the country's governance, knowledge and craftsmanship, with an audio guide in 5 languages. The 'Palace in Motion' show projects light and sound onto the palace facade each evening. Facilities: free parking, valet AED 35, stroller rental AED 20, free wheelchairs and a complimentary shuttle from the Visitor Centre. Knees and shoulders must be covered (no flip-flops); no cloakroom or lockers, and phones must be on silent inside.
qasr_al_watan.nudge: Stay for the evening 'Palace in Motion' light show projected onto the facade.

yas_island.title: Yas Island
yas_island.hours: Varies by park — e.g. Warner Bros. World 11:00–20:00; Ferrari World until 20:00; Yas Waterworld until 19:00; SeaWorld until 18:00; CLYMB until 22:00
yas_island.fee: Varies by park. Examples: Warner Bros. World AED 310 online (AED 345 gate); Yas Waterworld AED 270 adult; 3-park 3-day pass AED 545. Online tickets are usually ~10% cheaper than the gate
yas_island.context: Abu Dhabi's leisure and entertainment hub, home to Ferrari World (the world's fastest rollercoaster, 40+ rides), Warner Bros. World™ (29 rides across 6 themed lands), Yas Waterworld (60+ rides and slides), SeaWorld Abu Dhabi, and CLYMB™ (the world's largest indoor skydiving tunnel and tallest indoor climbing wall). Buy online to save ~10%; a 3-park 3-day pass is AED 545 and annual passes are available for residents.
yas_island.nudge: Bundle theme-park tickets in advance and use the free Yas Express shuttle between parks.

saadiyat_beach.title: Saadiyat Island — Beach & Cultural District
saadiyat_beach.hours: Public beach open daily; Cultural District museums vary (e.g. Louvre Tue–Sun from 10:00, closed Mondays)
saadiyat_beach.fee: Public beach: nominal facilities fee. Museums: Louvre AED 65, Natural History Museum AED 70, Zayed National Museum AED 70, teamLab Phenomena AED 150; Abrahamic Family House free
saadiyat_beach.context: A protected white-sand island with clear waters and nesting hawksbill turtles, set alongside the Saadiyat Cultural District — Louvre Abu Dhabi, the Natural History Museum, Zayed National Museum, teamLab Phenomena, the Abrahamic Family House (a mosque, church and synagogue on one site, free entry) and Manarat Al Saadiyat. A Museums Pass is the best value for 2+ venues; allow 1–2 hours per museum. Saadiyat Beach Golf Club is also on the island.
saadiyat_beach.nudge: Arrive early for parking and look out for nesting hawksbill turtles in season.

corniche.title: Abu Dhabi Corniche
corniche.fee: Free to walk and use the promenade; some beach facilities may charge
corniche.context: An 8 km beachfront promenade with cycling and jogging tracks and a white-sand public beach. The Founder's Memorial sits on Corniche Road (free entry, 09:00–22:00), and the Heritage Village is at the Breakwater end. Best visited early morning or at sunset.
corniche.nudge: Rent a bike along the cycle path and finish with sunset views over the skyline.

qasr_al_hosn.title: Qasr Al Hosn
qasr_al_hosn.fee: Outdoor areas, the House of Artisans and the Cultural Foundation are free; museum entry AED 30; special workshops ticketed separately
qasr_al_hosn.context: Abu Dhabi's oldest and most significant building (built 1761), now a national archive and museum of traditional Emirati crafts. The site includes the Abu Dhabi Children's Library (5,250 sqm across three floors) and Bait Al Gahwa, a traditional Arabic coffee ceremony experience. Reopened to the public in 2018 after a decade-long restoration, and host of the annual Qasr Al Hosn Festival.
qasr_al_hosn.nudge: Combine with the adjacent House of Artisans to see traditional Emirati crafts.

heritage_village.title: Emirates Heritage Village
heritage_village.location: Corniche Breakwater, near Marina Mall, Abu Dhabi
heritage_village.context: A reconstructed traditional village on the Breakwater with an old-world souk, a replica mosque and a Bedouin camp, plus live craft demonstrations — pottery, glassblowing, weaving and metalwork — that visitors can try themselves. A spice shop sells dried herbs, handmade soaps and souvenirs. Run by the Emirates Heritage Club, it offers excellent views of the Abu Dhabi skyline and the Arabian Gulf. Best visited early morning on weekdays; Marina Mall is nearby for parking and dining.
heritage_village.nudge: Visit the workshops to watch metalwork and pottery, then enjoy the breakwater views back toward the city.

airport_transfer.title: Zayed International Airport Transfers
airport_transfer.fee: Taxi flag fall AED 20 + AED 1.82/km (minimum AED 12); public bus route 040 to the city
airport_transfer.context: Zayed International Airport (AUH) is now open to non-travellers via an Airport Pass (no flight ticket needed). Estimated taxi fares: Abu Dhabi city centre AED 70–80 (~35–40 min), Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque AED 60–70 (~25 min), Yas Island AED 50–60 (~20 min), Dubai Downtown AED 300–400 (~90 min). Options include metered taxis, the public bus (route 040), Careem/Uber, and complimentary hotel shuttles.
airport_transfer.nudge: Pre-book a taxi or check route 040 bus timings before you land.

public_transport.title: Abu Dhabi Public Transport
public_transport.hours: Buses run on published daily schedules
public_transport.fee: AED 2 per local trip; pay with the Hafilat smart card only (cash and bank cards are not accepted on buses)
public_transport.context: Operated by Abu Dhabi Mobility (ITC). Pay with the rechargeable Hafilat card, bought and topped up at main stations, customer-service offices and kiosks. Local city routes 1–99 cost AED 2; suburban routes 100–199 cost AED 2 + AED 0.05/km; inter-emirate routes E100/E101 run to Dubai. 'Abu Dhabi Link' is an on-demand corner-to-corner bus (AED 2) covering Yas Island, Saadiyat Island, Khalifa City and Al Shahama. Ferries serve Dalma Island (AED 20 per passenger).
public_transport.nudge: Pick up a Hafilat card for buses and keep it topped up for seamless travel across the city.

visa_info.title: UAE Tourist Visa Information
visa_info.fee: Varies by nationality and visa type — check u.ae for current fees
visa_info.context: Many nationalities receive a visa on arrival or visa-free entry to the UAE. Visa types include tourist (available online, via airlines, travel agents or on arrival), transit, residence, and visit visas. Apply through an airline, travel agent or the ICA portal, and track applications on u.ae. An Emirates ID is required for UAE residents for most government and daily services.
visa_info.nudge: Confirm whether your nationality qualifies for visa-on-arrival before booking your trip.

culture_etiquette.title: Culture & Etiquette in Abu Dhabi
culture_etiquette.context: Dress modestly in public — cover shoulders and knees in malls, souks, government buildings and heritage sites; mosques require loose, long-sleeved clothing and women must cover their hair. Core rules: no swearing, rude gestures or public intoxication; keep public displays of affection minimal; always ask before photographing people; alcohol is served only in licensed venues; use the right hand for eating and handing items. Accepting Arabic coffee (gahwa) is a sign of respect. During Ramadan, eating, drinking and smoking in public during daylight is forbidden for everyone and dress should be even more conservative. On Fridays some attractions have reduced hours.
culture_etiquette.nudge: Dress modestly at religious and government sites and ask before photographing people.
